brief.audience: agent builders, applied AI researchers, forward-deployed engineers, technical founders and investors
brief.prompt:
By the end, a technical reviewer should understand that NodeKit is a portable application contract and verification path, see exactly which product slices have passed which gates, and know where the remaining release boundaries are local, blocked, or pending publication.
Every material claim must remain bound to the supplied Change Story evidence.
Both walkthrough videos are verified and hash-bound; publication remains a separate receipt gate.
https://github.com/HomenShum/node-platform/releases/tag/v0.2.0
https://github.com/HomenShum/agentic-rl-research-lab/releases/tag/v0.1.0-diagnostic-baselines
https://github.com/HomenShum/agentic-rl-research-lab/releases/tag/v0.1.0-diagnostic-baselines
https://www.anthropic.com/news/rare-disease-research-grants
https://medium.com/intuitionmachine/from-loop-engineering-to-graph-engineering-d3ebeb08511c
https://github.com/HomenShum/agentic-rl-research-lab/actions/runs/29799453044
https://founder-quest-graph.vercel.app/
https://github.com/HomenShum/founder-quest-graph/releases/tag/v0.1.1-production-certified
brief.purpose: By the end, a technical reviewer should understand that NodeKit is a portable application contract and verification path, see exactly which product slices have passed which gates, and know where the remaining release boundaries are local, blocked, or pending publication. Media-ready and not yet published.
successCriteria: strict paid-provider receipts with no fallback
browser journeys with console and network health
exact deployment identity and fresh-user production proof where claimed
editable deck export and reopen verification
public URL verification and distribution receipts
Bind Founder Quest production copy to E12 and E13
Keep the recursive publication claim in explicit future tense
Bind the exact verified video receipt and retain not-published distribution status

deck.title: NodeKit launches NodeKit through proof-carrying product work

elements.content: One manifest connects runtime, evaluation, browser QA, and proof.
elements.name: Body copy

elements.content: The hard part is keeping every proof gate on one execution path.
elements.name: Headline

elements.content: Parity breaks when benchmark, browser, deployment, and public claims follow different implementations.
elements.name: Body copy

elements.content: 01  One implementation must serve every gate
elements.name: Key point 1

elements.content: Two presets passed the full factory path in 37.221 seconds.
elements.name: Headline

elements.content: From empty directories, NodeKit installed, compiled, ran, evaluated, benchmarked, and proof-checked both presets.
elements.name: Body copy

elements.content: The Casca lab passed every local gate without making a credit decision.
elements.name: Headline

elements.content: On commit 6c8d7d9, the synthetic lab passed deterministic, live-provider, browser, reload, export, and identity gates.
elements.name: Body copy

math.description: Synthetic local workflow only; no automated credit decision.

elements.content: Protect the scorer first; train only after its boundaries are credible.
elements.name: Headline

elements.content: The current baseline is behavior cloning, not RL. It passed protected local evaluation; the strict Azure-only live route failed closed with no fallback.
elements.name: Body copy

elements.content: Founder Quest passed all 15 hosted checks on one exact production revision.
elements.name: Headline

elements.content: Read-only Founder Quest connects quests, a bounded graph, sourced answers, authority boundaries, and proof in four isolated browser contexts.
elements.name: Body copy

elements.altText: Founder Quest verified read-only production product view
elements.name: Editable image

elements.content: Production screenshot; verified scope remains synthetic and read-only.
elements.name: Image credit

elements.content: A real grant scenario tests the graph without claiming biological proof.
elements.name: Headline

elements.content: Anthropic's rare-disease grant is an independent forcing function for mapping research, novelty, evaluation, authority, and readiness, not an affiliation.
elements.name: Body copy

elements.content: 03  Research synthesis does not prove biology
elements.name: Key point 3

math.description: Research synthesis informs an application; it does not prove biology.

elements.content: Loops need external anchors. Bring one workflow and inspect the proof.
elements.name: Headline

elements.content: Research, build, evaluation, proof, presentation, and feedback can watch one another. Only external anchors close the graph.
elements.name: Body copy

elements.content: 03  Inspect pass, fail, and human-review status
elements.name: Key point 3

math.description: External sources, commits, artifacts, and observed outcomes close the loop.

slides.notes:
MEDIA-BOUND FINAL — publication remains a separate receipt gate.

Open on the outcome, not the component inventory. NodeKit is the portable contract
and certification path around an agent application. The factory receipt is the first
anchor.

Evidence IDs: E1_FACTORY_ACCEPTANCE, E2_NODEKIT_RELEASE.
slides.title: What is NodeKit?

slides.notes:
MEDIA-BOUND FINAL — publication remains a separate receipt gate.

Explain the failure class: a benchmark can call a different planner than production;
a UI can show a plausible answer without durable state; a release can cite a test
that was not run against the deployed revision. NodeKit compiles these paths from the
same definition and records where parity is still absent.

Evidence IDs: none; planned content only.
slides.title: What pain is this solving?

slides.notes:
MEDIA-BOUND FINAL — publication remains a separate receipt gate.

Use the exact 37.221-second deterministic acceptance result. Two independently
generated candidate repositories were installed, compiled twice for reproducibility,
committed, run, evaluated, benchmarked, and proof-checked. Do not call this a cloud or
production benchmark.

Evidence IDs: E1_FACTORY_ACCEPTANCE, E2_NODEKIT_RELEASE.
slides.title: Does the empty-directory path actually work?

slides.notes:
MEDIA-BOUND FINAL — publication remains a separate receipt gate.

Show the synthetic Casca FDE lab. Highlight graph-aware blocker explanation, proposal
review, reload persistence, one bounded live model call, export/reopen, and the explicit
absence of an automated credit decision. State clearly that deployment is the missing
release gate.

Evidence IDs: E3_CASCA_RELEASE, E4_CASCA_BROWSER, E5_CASCA_LIVE.
slides.title: Can the system survive a real agentic product journey?

slides.notes:
MEDIA-BOUND FINAL — publication remains a separate receipt gate.

The present baseline is behavior cloning, not RL. The important product result is the
step trajectory, protected reward, adversarial suite, heldout boundary, and a live route
that failed closed instead of silently weakening its privacy policy. The next scientific
milestone is a larger preregistered corpus and a shadow-only trained-policy comparison.

Evidence IDs: E6_RL_RELEASE, E7_RL_HELDOUT, E8_RL_LIVE_BLOCKED, E11_RL_CI.
slides.title: How does NodeKit approach Agentic RL responsibly?

slides.notes:
MEDIA-BOUND FINAL — publication remains a separate receipt gate.

The public read-only synthetic product passed all 15 hosted checks on source commit
dc5fba2467576d5ded3d3ac6bf4d142316410c86 and evidence commit
04579137a85f4ce18db61e6e7c0f25ff7b1d42b6. The same app/config hash is
973ea7cbd8039e963bab1ff604bc0180bf0457f9a334b9b9dc0f77062f103e20, and the graph
revision is ng1_c9334138. Four isolated desktop/mobile light/dark contexts recorded
zero browser, network, or cross-origin errors. The unified release receipt is
production-certified, release-ready, and hosted-deployment-certified; a fresh Windows
worktree verified all 25 artifact-manifest hashes byte-for-byte. The final independent
release re-audit found zero remaining issues, and the suite passed 18/18 tests. Keep
the boundary explicit: the journey is synthetic and read-only, with no durable writes
or remote Neo4j writes, and it does not prove external approval.

Evidence IDs: E12_FOUNDER_QUEST_PRODUCTION, E13_FOUNDER_QUEST_RELEASE.
slides.title: What does the graph make possible for a user?

slides.notes:
MEDIA-BOUND FINAL — publication remains a separate receipt gate.

Anthropic's rare-disease grant announcement is an external scenario, not an affiliation.
Explain how NodeKit would construct the latest-research ledger, novelty graph, benchmark,
Claude-credit plan, and application packet while keeping scientific and clinical authority
with qualified researchers and professionals.

Evidence IDs: E9_ANTHROPIC_GRANT_SOURCE.
slides.title: How would a researcher use this today?

slides.notes:
MEDIA-BOUND FINAL — publication remains a separate receipt gate.

Name the graph: research, build, evaluation, proof, presentation, distribution, feedback,
and memory. The graph is credible only when it resolves to current sources, exact commits,
fresh-browser evidence, exports, public URLs, and observed response. Invite researchers,
builders, and domain operators to bring one bounded consequential workflow. Close on a
falsifiable promise: the system should expose what passed, what failed, what is local,
and what still needs a human.

Evidence IDs: E1_FACTORY_ACCEPTANCE, E2_NODEKIT_RELEASE, E4_CASCA_BROWSER, E10_GRAPH_OF_LOOPS_SOURCE.
slides.title: How do the pieces improve, and what should reviewers do next?

Uploaded file: NodeKit slide design plans
{
  "communicationJob": "By the end, a technical reviewer should understand what NodeKit now proves, which real product slices support the claim, and which artifact and publication gates remain open.",
  "schemaVersion": "nodeslide.design-plan/v1",
  "slides": [
    {
      "audienceQuestion": "What is NodeKit?",
      "body": "One manifest connects runtime, evaluation, browser QA, and proof.",
      "bullets": [
        "Two presets; one path",
        "Local run: 37.221 s",
        "GitHub source; npm blocked"
      ],
      "claimIds": [
        "C1_FACTORY_ACCEPTANCE"
      ],
      "density": "low",
      "dominantVisual": "One clean lifecycle from brief to public proof with receipt checkpoints.",
      "evidenceIds": [
        "E1_FACTORY_ACCEPTANCE",
        "E2_NODEKIT_RELEASE"
      ],
      "id": "S1",
      "job": "Open with the system's concrete promise.",
      "narrativeRole": "opening",
      "takeaway": "Empty directory. Proof-carrying application."
    },
    {
      "audienceQuestion": "What pain is this solving?",
      "body": "Parity breaks when benchmark, browser, deployment, and public claims follow different implementations.",
      "bullets": [
        "One implementation must serve every gate",
        "Receipts bind the exact revision",
        "Failed gates stay visible"
      ],
      "claimIds": [],
      "density": "medium",
      "dominantVisual": "Fragmented parallel paths collapsing into one compiled definition.",
      "evidenceIds": [],
      "id": "S2",
      "job": "Show why code generation alone is insufficient.",
      "narrativeRole": "stakes",
      "takeaway": "The hard part is keeping every proof gate on one execution path."
    },
    {
      "audienceQuestion": "Does the empty-directory path actually work?",
      "body": "From empty directories, NodeKit installed, compiled, ran, evaluated, benchmarked, and proof-checked both presets.",
      "bullets": [
        "Agentic RL preset: passed",
        "SMB lending FDE preset: passed",
        "Local benchmark, not production latency"
      ],
      "claimIds": [
        "C1_FACTORY_ACCEPTANCE"
      ],
      "density": "medium",
      "dominantVisual": "Two receipt cards joined to one factory-acceptance digest.",
      "evidenceIds": [
        "E1_FACTORY_ACCEPTANCE",
        "E2_NODEKIT_RELEASE"
      ],
      "id": "S3",
      "job": "Prove the factory rather than describe it.",
      "metric": {
        "label": "deterministic local factory acceptance",
        "value": "37.221 s"
      },
      "narrativeRole": "evidence",
      "takeaway": "Two presets passed the full factory path in 37.221 seconds."
    },
    {
      "audienceQuestion": "Can the system survive a real agentic product journey?",
      "body": "On commit 6c8d7d9, the synthetic lab passed deterministic, live-provider, browser, reload, export, and identity gates.",
      "bullets": [
        "One bounded live-provider call",
        "Fresh browser plus reload",
        "Deployment gate still open"
      ],
      "claimIds": [
        "C2_CASCA_LOCAL_PROOF"
      ],
      "density": "medium",
      "dominantVisual": "Product screenshot annotated with graph query, proposal, and receipt checkpoints.",
      "evidenceIds": [
        "E3_CASCA_RELEASE",
        "E4_CASCA_BROWSER",
        "E5_CASCA_LIVE"
      ],
      "formula": {
        "description": "Synthetic local workflow only; no automated credit decision.",
        "display": "query → proposal → approval → export",
        "expression": "query -> proposal -> approval -> export"
      },
      "id": "S4",
      "job": "Show the consequential artifact vertical.",
      "narrativeRole": "case-study",
      "takeaway": "The Casca lab passed every local gate without making a credit decision."
    },
    {
      "audienceQuestion": "How does NodeKit approach Agentic RL responsibly?",
      "body": "The current baseline is behavior cloning, not RL. It passed protected local evaluation; the strict Azure-only live route failed closed with no fallback.",
      "bullets": [
        "Behavior cloning only",
        "Nine reward attacks blocked",
        "Azure/ZDR route: HTTP 404; no fallback"
      ],
      "chart": {
        "labels": [
          "reward attacks",
          "heldout tasks",
          "fallbacks"
        ],
        "unit": "count",
        "values": [
          9,
          2,
          0
        ]
      },
      "claimIds": [
        "C3_RL_LOCAL_BASELINE",
        "C4_RL_LIVE_ROUTE"
      ],
      "density": "high",
      "dominantVisual": "Trajectory to protected reward to shadow candidate flow, with behavior cloning labeled as the current stage.",
      "evidenceIds": [
        "E6_RL_RELEASE",
        "E7_RL_HELDOUT",
        "E8_RL_LIVE_BLOCKED",
        "E11_RL_CI"
      ],
      "id": "S5",
      "job": "Explain the research and learning boundary.",
      "metric": {
        "label": "adversarial reward attacks blocked",
        "value": "9"
      },
      "narrativeRole": "technical-decision",
      "takeaway": "Protect the scorer first; train only after its boundaries are credible."
    },
    {
      "audienceQuestion": "What does the graph make possible for a user?",
      "body": "Read-only Founder Quest connects quests, a bounded graph, sourced answers, authority boundaries, and proof in four isolated browser contexts.",
      "bullets": [
        "Source dc5fba2 · evidence 0457913",
        "App/config 973ea7cb · graph ng1_c9334138",
        "18 / 18 tests · 25 / 25 artifact hashes"
      ],
      "claimIds": [
        "C5_FOUNDER_QUEST_PRODUCT"
      ],
      "density": "medium",
      "dominantVisual": "Verified production Founder Quest view with quest rail, bounded graph, answer panel, and proof strip.",
      "evidenceIds": [
        "E12_FOUNDER_QUEST_PRODUCTION",
        "E13_FOUNDER_QUEST_RELEASE"
      ],
      "id": "S6",
      "job": "Introduce Founder Quest as the connected product surface.",
      "metric": {
        "label": "hosted checks passed; synthetic and read-only",
        "value": "15 / 15"
      },
      "narrativeRole": "product-reveal",
      "takeaway": "Founder Quest passed all 15 hosted checks on one exact production revision."
    },
    {
      "audienceQuestion": "How would a researcher use this today?",
      "body": "Anthropic's rare-disease grant is an independent forcing function for mapping research, novelty, evaluation, authority, and readiness, not an affiliation.",
      "bullets": [
        "Official grant announcement",
        "No affiliation or endorsement",
        "Research synthesis does not prove biology"
      ],
      "claimIds": [
        "C6_ANTHROPIC_GRANT_CASE"
      ],
      "density": "medium",
      "dominantVisual": "Disease-gene-phenotype-mechanism-paper-trial-hypothesis graph with source ledger.",
      "evidenceIds": [
        "E9_ANTHROPIC_GRANT_SOURCE"
      ],
      "formula": {
        "description": "Research synthesis informs an application; it does not prove biology.",
        "display": "source → claim → evaluation → application readiness",
        "expression": "source -> claim -> evaluation -> application readiness"
      },
      "id": "S7",
      "job": "Connect the external rare-disease grant scenario.",
      "narrativeRole": "external-use-case",
      "takeaway": "A real grant scenario tests the graph without claiming biological proof."
    },
    {
      "audienceQuestion": "How do the pieces improve, and what should reviewers do next?",
      "body": "Research, build, evaluation, proof, presentation, and feedback can watch one another. Only external anchors close the graph.",
      "bullets": [
        "Researcher, builder, or operator",
        "Sources, commits, exports, and outcomes",
        "Inspect pass, fail, and human-review status"
      ],
      "claimIds": [
        "C8_GRAPH_OF_LOOPS_FRAME"
      ],
      "density": "low",
      "dominantVisual": "Loop graph with external reality anchors and three reviewer entry points.",
      "evidenceIds": [
        "E1_FACTORY_ACCEPTANCE",
        "E2_NODEKIT_RELEASE",
        "E4_CASCA_BROWSER",
        "E10_GRAPH_OF_LOOPS_SOURCE"
      ],
      "formula": {
        "description": "External sources, commits, artifacts, and observed outcomes close the loop.",
        "display": "research ↔ build ↔ evaluation ↔ proof",
        "expression": "research <-> build <-> evaluation <-> proof"
      },
      "id": "S8",
      "job": "Explain the graph of loops and close with a falsifiable invitation.",
      "narrativeRole": "synthesis",
      "takeaway": "Loops need external anchors. Bring one workflow and inspect the proof."
    }
  ]
}
